title: cerastium glutinosum subsp. obscurum
intro: Cerastium glutinosum subsp. obscurum, commonly known as sticky mouse-ear or sticky mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cerastium glutinosum subsp. obscurum include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and support wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to promote health and well-being.
